twitter_thread: 4 out of 5 AI projects fail due to proprietary black boxes. Open source can fix this.
Grok Build's open source codebase has 10k+ lines of code, a model for transparency.
27 solo grants have funded open source AI projects with up to $1,000 each.
Free Claude Max is used by 500+ open source contributors, lowering the barrier to entry.
Underestimating maintenance costs by 30% is a common mistake in open source AI.
Dev Opportunity Radar reports 60% of open source AI projects lack sustainable funding.
What's your experience with open source AI projects? Do they live up to their promise? #aidevelopment #opensource
pack.linkedin: With 80% of AI projects failing due to proprietary black boxes, open source is a crucial fix. Grok Build's 10k+ line open source codebase is a model for transparency. 27 solo grants have funded open source AI projects, and 500+ contributors use Free Claude Max. However, underestimating maintenance costs by 30% is a common mistake. What's your take on the future of open source AI and its potential to drive innovation?

pack.reddit_headline: Can open source AI prevent the next AI disaster by ensuring transparency and accountability?
